title: Design Training
description: Train the skill of communicating feeling with color and shape!

scenes.speech: Welcome to the Design Dojo! Train to express feelings through color and shape! 🎨
scenes.title: Design Dojo
scenes.content: Design training trains less about drawing skill and more about designing how you want viewers to feel. Decide first 'who you're talking to' and 'what you want them to feel'.

scenes.title: Meaning of Colors
scenes.content: Red = passion / alert, blue = trust / calm, green = nature / safety, yellow = energy / attention. Pick a color that matches the feeling you want to share — viewers feel it instantly.
scenes.highlight:
Colors and feelings:
- Red = passion
- Blue = trust
- Green = safety
- Yellow = energy

scenes.title: 3-Color Rule
scenes.content: Stick to a main, sub, and accent color — three at most. Keeping the accent under 10% of the design makes it pop without overpowering.

scenes.speech: 'Whitespace' is part of the design too!
scenes.title: Power of Whitespace
scenes.content: Cramming everything in stops people from reading. Whitespace lets the eye rest and what you really want to show stand out. A little space around text and images changes the impression.
scenes.highlight:
Whitespace effects:
- Easy to read
- Important parts pop
- Looks premium

scenes.speech: Just 'aligning things' makes it look pro!
scenes.title: Magic of Alignment
scenes.content: Just aligning text and images cleanly to the left or center makes a design feel professional in an instant. The trick is to follow an invisible grid.
scenes.highlight:
Alignment:
- Left align
- Center align
- Follow the grid

scenes.speech: Use 'size contrast' to point out what matters!
scenes.title: Hierarchy by Size
scenes.content: Big title, small body. A clear contrast tells the viewer where to look first. If the contrast is small, everything seems equally important.
scenes.highlight:
Size contrast:
- Title: large
- Subhead: medium
- Body: small

scenes.speech: Today's challenge: 'design your own business card'!
scenes.title: My Business Card
scenes.content: Put name, what you love, and your dream on it. Apply the 3-color rule and alignment. Tools like Canva or Figma get you a result fast.
scenes.highlight:
3 things on the card:
- Name
- What you love
- Your dream

scenes.speech: Great work! Let's make the world prettier! 🌟
scenes.title: Training Complete!
scenes.content: Design is not just beauty; it's a tool that helps your message land. Show today's card to one person and ask what they feel.
